reviews.review_msg: This prof makes CMPT 320 more of a phil 101 course then a com science course. the final was ridiculous, asking very detail and useless information from his very unorganized notes,like "in which year, xyz said such such such?", I mean, who cares?!it's a computer science course!not a history course. do urself a favor and avoid him

This is exactly the same as any liberal arts course.  It was not a bad course but it is mandatory.  He doesn't post anything online, so go to class and take all his notes.  Study those and remember everything.  His essay was easy.  I want to take CMPT 418 with him.  Although he is an old hippy with a PHD in Philosophy and has left wing ideals.

He doesn't post notes on website because then nobody would come. Well of course not, it's a completely useless class (but mandatory). As previously said, his notes are very disorganized and his exams are for very specific details. e.g. "Which of the following was the author of xyz theory?" If possible find someone else who teaches this course.

Worst teacher i ever had. Notes totally disorganized. Writes so fast that you cant even catch up. Very confusing, and he expects you to have Very specific details on a book that put me to sleep. If you need to take cmpt 320. More work but WAY better teacher.

Taught useless PHIL 100 material that added no value. Had no problems reminding the class of his political opinions - I don't necessarily disagree with him, but it got old quite fast. Ridiculous final, 30 minutes long, required NO thought - just remembering absolutely USELESS facts ie MC what was the % decline of factory workers in 1999? Who CARES?

He's a good prof for this class.  His teaching style is what anyone in liberal arts courses might be used to so a bit different from CS, but it's an easy writing course.  Just write properly and in English and it's at least a B- from what I remember, especially since there's no specifically right answer always.

He's one of the best profs I've ever had. Refreshing to see a prof that actually cares about students beyond his course material. Yes he debunks some conspiracy theories in his class, he does that to show the importance of critical thinking and not following the herd. He has a unique sense of humor, and made boring topics very interesting.

He is extremely helpful. He stays behind class (even after this office hours) to talk and that is why he doesn't like discussions in class (time constraints stated in syllabus). His style of teachingtesting challenges your way of thought rather than the way you learn information.
He used five weeks for presentation. I literally learned nothing from this course.
Worst instructor ever. Taking this course is completely a waste of time.

Chris Bastone is the worst professor ever. He doesn't know how to teach, reading his notes all the classes and required a lot of pointless readings. And surprisingly, he didn't finish most of these readings himself. When students pop up some questions about reading, he refused to answer them. AVOID HIM!!
He is one of my best prof. He is very helpful. Too much reading for this course and TA has a problem communicate with students but the prof is really good. I loved this course and enjoyed this class.

Course was interesting; brought a new perspective to technology. Final exam prep was different than most classes. Lectures can be long and dry, but Baston does his best to make them interesting. Shows lots of videos in class.

This guy is probably one of the most useless professors i have ever have had the displeasure of had teach. He badgers with irrelevant personal stories, and has an AWFUL teaching method (where he reads from a piece of paper ALL CLASS). Class exercises are stupid, and TA is not great. My advice: shut off your brain, if  you think, itll drive you mad.
He is serious about teaching, but he is not good at it. His TA absolutely sucks.(worst TA ever had) On top of that, he himself isn't very helpful at all. Do not recommend him for cmpt320.
Enlightening and inspiring. Be prepared to read, research and write. Comp sci students need this perspective!

Professor Oliver is very enjoyable to listen to even though the course content is dry and boring. His exams are multiple-choice heavy so if you don't like those then avoid taking this class with him. Otherwise, he is a great 320 teacher.
Oliver is a good choice for those want to learn and enjoy the lecture. The midterm and final might be difficult or easy depends on how did you follow his schedule.
